intents.title: Single-source factual answer includes a grounded citation
intents.input: According to Lilian Weng, what are the two broad types of reward hacking?
intents.behavior_under_test: The assistant answers the factual question correctly and cites the reward hacking post rather than adding an unsupported source label.
intents.pass_criteria: The answer names environment hacking and training-process hacking, and the rubric should confirm that the cited source is the reward hacking post.
intents.failure_mode: The assistant answers correctly but omits the citation or cites the wrong post.
intents.probe_rationale: This is the simplest high-confidence regression guard for the new citation requirement.
intents.related_risk_flag: Retrieved context is assembled from raw page_content only, so the model may fabricate or misattribute citations when chunks are ambiguous.
native_shape_notes: Preserve the existing LangSmith dataset-example shape.
Store the grading rubric in metadata, not in a separate generic probe field.

intents.title: Cross-post answer cites the right posts for each claim
intents.input: Compare what the hallucination post says about FActScore with what the diffusion-video post says about Lumiere.
intents.behavior_under_test: The assistant keeps the two source posts distinct and attributes each claim to the correct post.
intents.pass_criteria: The rubric should confirm that FActScore is attributed to the hallucination post and Lumiere is attributed to the diffusion-video post without collapsing them into a single source.
intents.failure_mode: The assistant conflates the two posts or cites the wrong source for one side of the comparison.
intents.probe_rationale: This stresses the multi-source attribution boundary introduced by the citation instruction.
intents.related_risk_flag: Cross-post attribution may fail when multiple blog posts are retrieved together.
native_shape_notes: Use a judge rubric that explicitly grades attribution separation across posts.

intents.title: Ambiguous source question should avoid fabricated citation
intents.input: Which Lilian Weng post talks about temporal consistency, and what does it say?
intents.behavior_under_test: The assistant avoids inventing a source label when the retrieved text does not make the source unambiguous.
intents.pass_criteria: The rubric should confirm that the answer either cites the correct post with strong evidence or explicitly states uncertainty rather than fabricating an attribution.
intents.failure_mode: The assistant fabricates a confident citation to the wrong post.
intents.probe_rationale: This tests whether the new citation requirement overcorrects into unsupported source claims.
intents.related_risk_flag: The model may fabricate a source label when the retrieved text does not make the originating post identifiable.
native_shape_notes: Judge for calibrated uncertainty, not forced exactness.
